{{ version }} {{ conda-version }} {{ workflow-status }} {{ codecov }} {{ pypi-downloads }} {{ conda-downloads }} {{ license }} {{ matrix }}

czml3 is a Python library to write CZML.

# What is CZML?

From the official
[CZML Guide](https://github.com/AnalyticalGraphicsInc/czml-writer/wiki/CZML-Guide):

> CZML is a JSON format for describing a time-dynamic graphical scene,
> primarily for display in a web browser running Cesium.
> It describes lines, points, billboards, models, and other graphical primitives,
> and specifies how they change with time.
> While Cesium has a rich client-side API,
> CZML allows it to be data-driven
> so that a generic Cesium viewer can display a rich scene
> without the need for any custom code.

# Installation

You can install czml3 using pip:

```
$ pip install czml3
```

or conda:

```
$ conda install czml3 --channel conda-forge
```

czml3 requires Python >= 3.10.

# Examples

A CZML document is a list of *packets*, which have several properties.
When using czml3 in an interactive interpreter,
all objects show as nice CZML (JSON):

```
>>> from czml3 import Packet
>>> print(Packet())
{
    "id": "adae4d3a-7087-4fda-a70b-d18a262a890e"
}
>>> packet0 = Packet(id="Facility/AGI", name="AGI")
>>> print(packet0)
{
    "id": "Facility/AGI",
    "name": "AGI"
}
>>> packet0.dumps()
'{"id":"Facility/AGI","name":"AGI"}'
```

And there are more complex examples available:

```
>>> from czml3.examples import simple
>>> print(simple)
[
    {
        "id": "document",
        "version": "1.0",
        "name": "simple",
        "clock": {
            "interval": "2012-03-15T10:00:00Z/2012-03-16T10:00:00Z",
            "currentTime": "2012-03-15T10:00:00Z",
            "multiplier": 60
        }
    },
...
```

## Jupyter widget

You can easily display your CZML document using our interactive widget:

```
In [1]: from czml3.examples import simple

In [2]: from czml3.widget import CZMLWidget

In [3]: CZMLWidget(simple)
```

And this would be the result:

```{image} https://raw.githubusercontent.com/poliastro/czml3/master/widget-screenshot.png
```

# Support

{{ matrix }}

If you find any issue on czml3 or have questions,
please [open an issue on our repository](https://github.com/poliastro/czml3/issues/new)
and join [our chat]!

# Contributing

You want to contribute? Awesome! There are lots of
[CZML properties](https://github.com/AnalyticalGraphicsInc/czml-writer/wiki/Packet)
that we still did not implement. Also, it would be great to have
better validation, a Cesium widget in Jupyter notebook and JupyterLab...
Ideas welcome!

We recommend [this GitHub workflow](https://www.asmeurer.com/git-workflow/)
to fork the repository. To run the tests,
use [tox](https://tox.readthedocs.io/):

```
$ tox
```

Before you send us a pull request, remember to reformat all the code:

```
$ tox -e reformat
```

This will apply ruff and lots of love ❤️

# License

{{ license }}

czml3 is released under the MIT license, hence allowing commercial
use of the library. Please refer to the {code}`LICENSE` file.

classes.name: czml3.common.Deletable
documentation: A property whose value may be deleted.

classes.name: czml3.common.Interpolatable
documentation:
A property whose value may be determined by interpolating.

The interpolation happens over provided time-tagged samples.

classes.name: czml3.properties.HasAlignment
documentation: A property that can be horizontally or vertically aligned.

classes.name: czml3.properties.Material

class_variables.name: czml3.properties.Material.polylineOutline
documentation: NOTE: Not present in documentation
A definition of how a surface is colored or shaded.

classes.name: czml3.properties.PolylineOutline
documentation: A definition of how a surface is colored or shaded.

classes.name: czml3.properties.PolylineOutlineMaterial
documentation: A definition of the material wrapper for a polyline outline.

classes.name: czml3.properties.PolylineGlow
documentation: A definition of how a glowing polyline appears.

classes.name: czml3.properties.PolylineGlowMaterial
documentation: A material that fills the surface of a line with a glowing color.

classes.name: czml3.properties.PolylineArrow
documentation: A definition of how a polyline arrow appears.

classes.name: czml3.properties.PolylineArrowMaterial
documentation: A material that fills the surface of a line with an arrow.

classes.name: czml3.properties.PolylineDash
documentation: A definition of how a polyline should be dashed with two colors.

classes.name: czml3.properties.PolylineDashMaterial
documentation: A material that provides a how a polyline should be dashed.

classes.name: czml3.properties.PolylineMaterial
documentation: A definition of how a surface is colored or shaded.

classes.name: czml3.properties.SolidColorMaterial
documentation: A material that fills the surface with a solid color.

classes.name: czml3.properties.GridMaterial
documentation: A material that fills the surface with a two-dimensional grid.

classes.name: czml3.properties.StripeMaterial
documentation: A material that fills the surface with alternating colors.

classes.name: czml3.properties.CheckerboardMaterial
documentation: A material that fills the surface with alternating colors.

classes.name: czml3.properties.ImageMaterial
documentation: A material that fills the surface with an image.

classes.name: czml3.properties.Color
documentation: A color. The color can optionally vary over time.

classes.name: czml3.properties.Position
documentation: Defines a position. The position can optionally vary over time.

classes.name: czml3.properties.ViewFrom
documentation:
suggested initial camera position offset when tracking this object.

ViewFrom can optionally vary over time.

classes.name: czml3.properties.Billboard
documentation:
A billboard, or viewport-aligned image.

The billboard is positioned in the scene by the position property.
A billboard is sometimes called a marker.

classes.name: czml3.properties.EllipsoidRadii
documentation: The radii of an ellipsoid.

classes.name: czml3.properties.Corridor
documentation:
A corridor , which is a shape defined by a centerline and width that conforms to the
curvature of the body shape. It can can optionally be extruded into a volume.

classes.name: czml3.properties.Cylinder
documentation: A cylinder, which is a special cone defined by length, top and bottom radius.

classes.name: czml3.properties.Ellipse
documentation: An ellipse, which is a close curve, on or above Earth's surface.

classes.name: czml3.properties.Polygon

class_variables.name: czml3.properties.Polygon.holes
documentation: NOTE: not in documentation
A polygon, which is a closed figure on the surface of the Earth.

classes.name: czml3.properties.Polyline

type.name:
(
    None
    | PolylineMaterial
    | PolylineDashMaterial
    | PolylineArrowMaterial
    | PolylineGlowMaterial
    | PolylineOutlineMaterial
    | str
)

(
    None
    | PolylineMaterial
    | PolylineDashMaterial
    | PolylineArrowMaterial
    | PolylineGlowMaterial
    | PolylineOutlineMaterial
    | str
)

documentation: A polyline, which is a line in the scene composed of multiple segments.

classes.name: czml3.properties.ArcType
documentation: The type of an arc.

classes.name: czml3.properties.ShadowMode
documentation: Whether or not an object casts or receives shadows from each light source when shadows are enabled.

classes.name: czml3.properties.ClassificationType
documentation: Whether a classification affects terrain, 3D Tiles, or both.

classes.name: czml3.properties.DistanceDisplayCondition
documentation: Indicates the visibility of an object based on the distance to the camera.

classes.name: czml3.properties.PositionListOfLists
documentation: A list of positions.

classes.name: czml3.properties.PositionList

class_variables.name: czml3.properties.PositionList.epoch
documentation: note: not documented
A list of positions.

classes.name: czml3.properties.Ellipsoid
documentation: A closed quadric surface that is a three-dimensional analogue of an ellipse.

classes.name: czml3.properties.Box
documentation: A box, which is a closed rectangular cuboid.

classes.name: czml3.properties.BoxDimensions
documentation: The width, depth, and height of a box.

classes.name: czml3.properties.Rectangle
documentation:
A cartographic rectangle, which conforms to the curvature of the globe and
can be placed on the surface or at altitude and can optionally be extruded into a volume.

classes.name: czml3.properties.RectangleCoordinates
documentation: A set of coordinates describing a cartographic rectangle on the surface of the ellipsoid.

classes.name: czml3.properties.EyeOffset
documentation:
An offset in eye coordinates which can optionally vary over time.

Eye coordinates are a left-handed coordinate system
where the X-axis points toward the viewer's right,
the Y-axis poitns up, and the Z-axis points into the screen.

classes.name: czml3.properties.HeightReference
documentation: The height reference of an object, which indicates if the object's position is relative to terrain or not.

classes.name: czml3.properties.ColorBlendMode
documentation: The height reference of an object, which indicates if the object's position is relative to terrain or not.

classes.name: czml3.properties.CornerType
documentation: The height reference of an object, which indicates if the object's position is relative to terrain or not.

classes.name: czml3.properties.Clock
documentation:
Initial settings for a simulated clock when a document is loaded.

The start and stop time are configured using the interval property.

classes.name: czml3.properties.Path
documentation:
A path, which is a polyline defined by the motion of an object over time.

The possible vertices of the path are specified by the position property.
Note that because clients cannot render a truly infinite path,
the path must be limited,
either by defining availability for this object,
or by using the leadTime and trailTime properties.

classes.name: czml3.properties.Point
documentation: A point, or viewport-aligned circle.

classes.name: czml3.properties.Tileset
documentation: A 3D Tiles tileset.

classes.name: czml3.properties.Wall
documentation:
A two-dimensional wall defined as a line strip and optional maximum and minimum heights.
It conforms to the curvature of the globe and can be placed along the surface or at altitude.

classes.name: czml3.properties.NearFarScalar
documentation:
A numeric value which will be linearly interpolated between two values based on an object's distance from the
 camera, in eye coordinates.

The computed value will interpolate between the near value and the far value while the camera distance falls
between the near distance and the far distance, and will be clamped to the near or far value while the distance is
less than the near distance or greater than the far distance, respectively.

classes.name: czml3.properties.Label
documentation: A string of text.

classes.name: czml3.properties.Orientation
documentation:
Defines an orientation.

An orientation is a rotation that takes a vector expressed in the "body" axes of the object
and transforms it to the Earth fixed axes.

classes.name: czml3.properties.Model
documentation: A 3D model.

classes.name: czml3.properties.Uri
documentation:
A URI value.

The URI can optionally vary with time.

classes.name: czml3.core.Preamble
documentation: The preamble packet.

classes.name: czml3.core.Packet
documentation:
A CZML Packet.

See https://github.com/AnalyticalGraphicsInc/czml-writer/wiki/Packet
for further information.

classes.name: czml3.core.Document
documentation: A CZML document, consisting on a list of packets.

classes.name: czml3.enums.OCaseStrEnum
documentation: StrEnum where enum.auto() returns the original member name, not lower-cased name.

classes.name: czml3.enums.InterpolationAlgorithms
documentation: The interpolation algorithm to use when interpolating.

classes.name: czml3.enums.ExtrapolationTypes
documentation: The type of extrapolation to perform when a value is requested at a time after any available samples.

classes.name: czml3.enums.ReferenceFrames
documentation: The reference frame in which cartesian positions are specified.

classes.name: czml3.enums.LabelStyles
documentation: The style of a label.

classes.name: czml3.enums.ClockRanges
documentation: The behavior of a clock when its current time reaches its start or end time.

functions.name: czml3.types.get_color
documentation: Determines if the input is a valid color

classes.name: czml3.types.FontValue
documentation: A font, specified using the same syntax as the CSS "font" property.

classes.name: czml3.types.RgbafValue
documentation:
A color specified as an array of color components [Red, Green, Blue, Alpha]
 where each component is in the range 0.0-1.0. If the array has four elements,
the color is constant. If it has five or more elements, they are time-tagged
samples arranged as [Time, Red, Green, Blue, Alpha, Time, Red, Green, Blue, Alpha, ...],
where Time is an ISO 8601 date and time string or seconds since epoch.

classes.name: czml3.types.RgbaValue
documentation:
A color specified as an array of color components [Red, Green, Blue, Alpha]
where each component is in the range 0-255. If the array has four elements,
the color is constant.

If it has five or more elements, they are time-tagged samples arranged as
[Time, Red, Green, Blue, Alpha, Time, Red, Green, Blue, Alpha, ...], where Time
is an ISO 8601 date and time string or seconds since epoch.

classes.name: czml3.types.ReferenceValue
documentation:
Represents a reference to another property. References can be used to specify that two properties on different
objects are in fact, the same property.

classes.name: czml3.types.Cartesian3Value
documentation:
A three-dimensional Cartesian value specified as [X, Y, Z].

If the values has three elements, the value is constant.
If it has four or more elements, they are time-tagged samples
arranged as [Time, X, Y, Z, Time, X, Y, Z, ...],
where Time is an ISO 8601 date and time string or seconds since epoch.

classes.name: czml3.types.Cartesian2Value
documentation:
A two-dimensional Cartesian value specified as [X, Y].

If the values has two elements, the value is constant.
If it has three or more elements, they are time-tagged samples
arranged as [Time, X, Y, Time, X, Y, ...],
where Time is an ISO 8601 date and time string or seconds since epoch.

classes.name: czml3.types.CartographicRadiansValue
documentation:
A geodetic, WGS84 position specified as [Longitude, Latitude, Height].

Longitude and Latitude are in radians and Height is in meters.
If the array has three elements, the value is constant.
If it has four or more elements, they are time-tagged samples
arranged as [Time, Longitude, Latitude, Height, Time, Longitude, Latitude, Height, ...],
where Time is an ISO 8601 date and time string or seconds since epoch.

classes.name: czml3.types.CartographicDegreesValue
documentation:
A geodetic, WGS84 position specified as [Longitude, Latitude, Height].

Longitude and Latitude are in degrees and Height is in meters.
If the array has three elements, the value is constant.
If it has four or more elements, they are time-tagged samples
arranged as [Time, Longitude, Latitude, Height, Time, Longitude, Latitude, Height, ...],
where Time is an ISO 8601 date and time string or seconds since epoch.

classes.name: czml3.types.StringValue
documentation:
A string value.

The string can optionally vary with time.

classes.name: czml3.types.CartographicRadiansListValue
documentation:
A list of geodetic, WGS84 positions specified as [Longitude, Latitude, Height, Longitude, Latitude, Height, ...],
where Longitude and Latitude are in radians and Height is in meters.

classes.name: czml3.types.CartographicDegreesListValue
documentation:
A list of geodetic, WGS84 positions specified as [Longitude, Latitude, Height, Longitude, Latitude, Height, ...],
where Longitude and Latitude are in degrees and Height is in meters.

classes.name: czml3.types.DistanceDisplayConditionValue
documentation:
A value indicating the visibility of an object based on the distance to the camera, specified as two values
[NearDistance, FarDistance]. If the array has two elements, the value is constant. If it has three or more elements,
they are time-tagged samples arranged as [Time, NearDistance, FarDistance, Time, NearDistance, FarDistance, ...],
where Time is an ISO 8601 date and time string or seconds since epoch.

classes.name: czml3.types.NearFarScalarValue
documentation:
A near-far scalar value specified as four values [NearDistance, NearValue, FarDistance, FarValue].

 If the array has four elements, the value is constant. If it has five or more elements, they are time-tagged
samples arranged as [Time, NearDistance, NearValue, FarDistance, FarValue, Time, NearDistance, NearValue,
FarDistance, FarValue, ...], where Time is an ISO 8601 date and time string or seconds since epoch.

classes.name: czml3.types.TimeInterval
documentation: A time interval, specified in ISO8601 interval format.

classes.name: czml3.types.IntervalValue
documentation: Value over some interval.

classes.name: czml3.types.Sequence
documentation: Sequence, list, array of objects.

classes.name: czml3.types.UnitQuaternionValue
documentation:
A set of 4-dimensional coordinates used to represent rotation in 3-dimensional space.

It's specified as [X, Y, Z, W]. If the array has four elements, the value is constant.
If it has five or more elements, they are time-tagged samples arranged as
[Time, X, Y, Z, W, Time, X, Y, Z, W, ...],
where Time is an ISO 8601 date and time string or seconds since epoch.

classes.name: czml3.types.EpochValue
documentation: A value representing a time epoch.

classes.name: czml3.types.NumberValue
documentation: A single number, or a list of number pairs signifying the time and representative value.
